request.question: What island that Zanthoxylum hawaiiense can be found on is nicknamed“The Friendly Isle”?

title: Chelerythrine
Nitidine
Zanthoxylum psammophilum
Molokai
List of islands in Isle Royale National Park
Little Deer Isle, Maine
Zanthoxylum gilletii
Fagaronine
Zanthoxylum hawaiiense
Molokai Island Times
sentences: Chelerythrine is a benzophenanthridine alkaloid present in the plant "Chelidonium majus" (greater celandine).
It is a potent, selective, and cell-permeable protein kinase C inhibitor "in vitro".
And an efficacious antagonist of G-protein-coupled CB1 receptors.
It is also found in the plants "Zanthoxylum clava-herculis" and "Zanthoxylum rhoifolium", exhibiting antibacterial activity against "Staphylococcus aureus" and other human pathogens.
Nitidine is a benzophenanthridine alkaloid found in species of the genus "Zanthoxylum ", notably in "Zanthoxylum nitidum".
This compound has an anti-malarial activity.
Zanthoxylum psammophilum (synonym Fagara psammophila Aké Assi) is a species of shrub or small tree in the Rutaceae family.
It is a large liana endemic to Côte d'Ivoire, although in 2005 it was found in Liberia as well.

"Zanthoxylum psammophilum", a new combination created in 1975 to subsume the genus "Fagara" into the genus "Zanthoxylum" based on morphology and secondary metabolites, is the preferred name according to the Conservatoire et Jardin botaniques de la Ville de Genève which has a section that specializes in the conservation and biodiversity of flowering plants of Côte d'Ivoire.
However, the International Plant Names Index lists "Fagara" as the genus, and "Zanthoxylum" as the synonym.
The type specimen for the plant was collected in the ecotone between the lowland Eastern Guinean forests (tropical rainforest) and the inland Guinean forest-savanna mosaic in the Lagunes District.
Molokai ( ; Hawaiian: ] ), nicknamed “The Friendly Isle” is the fifth largest island of eight major islands that make up the Hawaiian Island Chain in the middle of the Pacific Ocean.

It is 38 by 10 miles (61 by 16 km) in size at its extreme length and width with a usable land area of 260 sqmi , making it the fifth-largest of the main Hawaiian Islands and the 27th largest island in the United States.
It lies east of Oahu across the 25-mile (40 km) wide Kaiwi Channel and north of Lānai, separated from it by the Kalohi Channel.
The following is a list of islands in Isle Royale National Park.
Located in Lake Superior, Isle Royale is the second largest island in the Great Lakes system, after only Manitoulin Island in Lake Huron.
At 206.7 mi² (535.4 km²), Isle Royale is the third largest island in the contiguous United States after Long Island and Padre Island.
The island and the surrounding islands and waters make up Isle Royale National Park for a total area of 209 sq mi² (542 km²) of above-water territory.
Little Deer Isle is an island in Penobscot Bay, lying just north of the town (and island) of Deer Isle, of which it is a part.
The island is served by Maine State Route 15 via the Deer Isle Bridge.
The north end of the island is called Eggemoggin and there is a small island with a lighthouse on it at the tip called Pumpkin Island.
Little Deer is about 4 miles long and connects to Deer Isle by way of a causeway.
There are only about 300 year-round residents of Little Deer, but many more summer residents.
Zanthoxylum gilletii, the East African satin wood, is a tree species in the genus "Zanthoxylum" found in Africa.
The fruits are used to produce the spice uzazi.
Fagaronine is a benzophenanthridine alkaloid found in "Zanthoxylum zanthoxyloides" and other species in the "Zanthoxylum" genus.
Zanthoxylum hawaiiense, commonly known Aʻ e or Hawaiʻ i pricklyash, is a species of flowering plant in the citrus family, Rutaceae, that is endemic to Hawaii.
It can be found at elevations of 550 – in dry forests, where it grows on lava flows, and mixed mesic forests on the Island of Hawaiʻ i, Maui, Molokaʻ i, and Lānaʻ i.
It is threatened by habitat loss.
The Molokai Island Times was one of three newspapers on the Hawaiian island of Moloka'i.

It was founded in December 2004 by Brennan Purtzer and Darrell Williams with a subsidy from computer software guru John McAfee, Williams' English cousin, and served as the newest community paper for "The Friendly Isle" of Molokai from 2005–2006, before becoming known as The Molokai Times in January 2007.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What island that Zanthoxylum hawaiiense can be found on is nicknamed“The Friendly Isle”?
hop1_docs: Zanthoxylum hawaiiense | Zanthoxylum hawaiiense, commonly known Aʻ e or Hawaiʻ i pricklyash, is a species of flowering plant in the citrus family, Rutaceae, that is endemic to Hawaii.  It can be found at elevations of 550 – in dry forests, where it grows on lava flows, and mixed mesic forests on the Island of Hawaiʻ i, Maui, Molokaʻ i, and Lānaʻ i.  It is threatened by habitat loss.

Zanthoxylum thomasianum | Zanthoxylum thomasianum, also known as the St. Thomas prickly-ash, is a species of plant in the Rutaceae family.  It is found in Puerto Rico, the British Virgin Islands, and the United States Virgin Islands.  Its natural habitats are tropical and subtropical dry broadleaf forests and shrublands.  It is threatened by habitat loss, and is the only on St. John listed as "endangered".

Lighthouses on the Isle of Arran | Three lighthouses can be found around the shores of the island of Arran in the Firth of Clyde.  One is located on the Isle of Pladda in the South and the other two can be found on Holy Isle in Lamlash Bay to the East of Arran.  All three are still in service and maintained by the Northern Lighthouse Board.  They are now fully automatic since the electrification of both lighthouses on Holy Isle in 1977.

Zanthoxylum kauaense | Zanthoxylum kauaense, commonly known as Aʻ e or Kauaʻ i pricklyash, is a species of flowering plant in the citrus family, Rutaceae, that is endemic to Hawaii.  It usually inhabits mixed mesic forests at elevations of 300 - , but can also be found in dry and wet forests.  It is threatened by habitat loss.

Zanthoxylum coreanum | The Korean lime tree, Zanthoxylum coreanum Nakai, is a rare shrub that only grows in Korea and China.  In Korea it is called wang-cho-pi tree and is used for many purposes.  There are researches that are done to figure out what compounds are in the "Zanthoxylum coreanum" Nakai and the uses of this plant.

Molokai | Molokai ( ; Hawaiian: ] ), nicknamed “The Friendly Isle” is the fifth largest island of eight major islands that make up the Hawaiian Island Chain in the middle of the Pacific Ocean.  It is 38 by 10 miles (61 by 16 km) in size at its extreme length and width with a usable land area of 260 sqmi , making it the fifth-largest of the main Hawaiian Islands and the 27th largest island in the United States.  It lies east of Oahu across the 25-mile (40 km) wide Kaiwi Channel and north of Lānai, separated from it by the Kalohi Channel.

Shaky Isles | The Shaky Isles or Shaky Islands is a nickname for New Zealand.  At one time this nickname was used in New Zealand itself, though its usage there is now seen as dated; it is still fairly widely used in Australia.

response.summary_1: Passage [1]: Zanthoxylum hawaiiense "can be found ... on the Island of Hawaiʻi, Maui, Molokaʻi, and Lānaʻi." Passage [6]: "Molokai ... nicknamed 'The Friendly Isle'." Bridge entity: Molokaʻi / Molokai — confirm spelling variant if needed. Evidence anchors for retrieval: the quoted island list in passage [1] and the Molokai page line "nicknamed 'The Friendly Isle'."
hop2_docs: Molokai | Molokai ( ; Hawaiian: ] ), nicknamed “The Friendly Isle” is the fifth largest island of eight major islands that make up the Hawaiian Island Chain in the middle of the Pacific Ocean.  It is 38 by 10 miles (61 by 16 km) in size at its extreme length and width with a usable land area of 260 sqmi , making it the fifth-largest of the main Hawaiian Islands and the 27th largest island in the United States.  It lies east of Oahu across the 25-mile (40 km) wide Kaiwi Channel and north of Lānai, separated from it by the Kalohi Channel.

Molokai Island Times | The Molokai Island Times was one of three newspapers on the Hawaiian island of Moloka'i.  It was founded in December 2004 by Brennan Purtzer and Darrell Williams with a subsidy from computer software guru John McAfee, Williams' English cousin, and served as the newest community paper for "The Friendly Isle" of Molokai from 2005–2006, before becoming known as The Molokai Times in January 2007.

Shaky Isles | The Shaky Isles or Shaky Islands is a nickname for New Zealand.  At one time this nickname was used in New Zealand itself, though its usage there is now seen as dated; it is still fairly widely used in Australia.
Molokai Ka Hula Piko | Molokai Ka Hula Piko is an annual festival of the hula, held on Molokai of Hawaii, U.S.

Corporation of the Sons and Friends of the Clergy | The Corporation of the Sons and Friends of the Clergy is a charity resulting from an amalgamation in 2013.  The two parent organisations date from 1655 and 1820, respectively.  It exists to provide financial support to clergy of the Anglican Communion who are diocesan clergy in a diocese of the British Isles (including the United Kingdom and Ireland, and the Crown dependencies of the Channel Islands and the Isle of Man) or missionary clergy serving overseas with a society based in one of those countries.  It also provides assistance to clergy widows, children, and orphans, and to members of Anglican religious orders within the British Isles.

Molokai: The Story of Father Damien | Molokai: The Story of Father Damien is a 1999 biographical film of Father Damien, a Belgian priest working at the Kalaupapa Leprosy Settlement on the Hawaiian island of Molokai.

West Molokai Volcano | West Molokai Volcano, sometimes called Mauna Loa for the census-designated place, is an extinct shield volcano comprising the western half of Molokai island in the U.S. state of Hawaii.

Molokai: The Story of Father Damien
response.summary_2: Zanthoxylum hawaiiense occurs on Molokaʻi (Molokai), and Molokai is nicknamed "The Friendly Isle."
